question.text: Under SEBI SAST Regulations 2011, which of the following correctly defines "control"?

answer.text: Holding more than 50% of the paid-up share capital of a company
The right to appoint majority of directors or to control management or policy decisions
Being appointed as a director or officer of the company
Holding the largest single block of shares in the company

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under SAST 2011, "control" means the right to appoint a majority of directors OR to control management/policy decisions, whether directly or indirectly, through shareholding, management rights, shareholders agreements, or voting agreements. **The Trap:** A director or officer is explicitly stated as NOT being in control merely by virtue of holding that position. Also, holding 50%+ shares does not automatically equal "control" under the SAST definition — it is about the right to appoint majority directors or control decisions. **Cross-Exam Context:** The definition of "control" is tested across NISM Series IX and is also relevant in NISM Series V-B (Mutual Fund) for understanding controlling interests in AMCs. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Control can exist even without majority shareholding — shareholders agreements, voting agreements, and management rights can confer control. Always look beyond the cap table.
